resposta:
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def fizzBuzz(self, n: int) -> List[str]:
        return [
            "FizzBuzz" if i % 15 == 0 else
            "Fizz" if i % 3 == 0 else
            "Buzz" if i % 5 == 0 else
            str(i)
            for i in range(1, n + 1)
        ]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Single pass list-comprehension with constant-time checks for divisibility by 15, 3, and 5; minimal CPU and memory usage.
